partsUsed: Essential oil distilled from leaves and terminal branches
habitat: Coastal northeastern New South Wales and southeastern Queensland, Australia; swampy ground; cultivated for oil.
traditionalUsesEn: Aboriginal Australian use of crushed leaves for skin complaints; modern topical antiseptic oil for minor cuts, acne, and fungal nail issues (diluted).
traditionalUsesDe: Traditionelle Nutzung zerdrückter Blätter durch Aborigines bei Hautbeschwerden; modernes topisches Antiseptikum (verdünnt) bei kleinen Wunden, Akne und Nagelpilz.
activeCompounds: Essential oil: terpinen-4-ol (key antimicrobial marker), γ-terpinene, α-terpinene; 1,8-cineole ideally low in medicinal-grade oil
preparation: Steam-distilled essential oil diluted in carrier oil or formulated products; never undiluted on large skin areas.
cautions: Toxic if swallowed. Can cause skin irritation or allergic contact dermatitis. Keep away from pets (especially cats). Use diluted.
historicalNotes: Commercial oil industry developed in Australia in the 20th century; ISO standards define oil quality (terpinen-4-ol content).
